mechanism: Conjunctive task performance under asymmetric ability: Weaker participant receives feedback of superior partner performance, increasing effort duration; verbal encouragement modulates this response. Estimated effort compared against baseline individual control.

Single-nucleotide polymorphism (SNP) frequency filtering: Extract non-synonymous coding SNPs from genome-wide genotypes. Compute frequency ratio between disease cohort and reference population. Select candidates meeting threshold (10% frequency + 2-fold difference). Functional annotation via pathway enrichment using hypergeometric distribution. CADD scoring quantifies deleterious potential (top 10% threshold).

Sentence unscrambling priming task manipulating cognitive resource allocation via instruction variance. Participants rating moral scenarios on 10-point severity scale. Response effort measured by task completion time or directly instructed. Two-way ANOVA contrasts priming condition (cleanliness vs neutral) and effort level (low vs high).

Preregistered randomized controlled trial: Intervention training embeds instructional design (4C/ID) targeting situational awareness, tactical gaze control, visual attention via video + practical exercises. Control training emphasizes speed-accuracy trade-off. Both groups perform pre/post video shoot/don't-shoot scenarios and marksmanship tasks. Eye-tracking records gaze timing. Repeated measures ANOVA on response times, hit timing, muzzle position, eye closure duration.

Eriksen flanker task variant: Visual target letters (H/S) displayed with irrelevant auditory flanker letters. Stimulus onset asynchrony (SOA) varies 0/250/500ms. Congruency conditions: perceptual-congruent (same letter), response-congruent (different letters, same response), incongruent (different response). Measure reaction time and accuracy. Compute Vincentized percentile RT distributions and conditional accuracy functions across bins.
